dailyJudgementSnippet: Google Gemini had the clearest positive day: Gemma 4 was repeatedly praised as a surprisingly strong local coding model, and Gemini also picked up a clean benchmark-style success mention, with only a softer caveat around tool use and large-context tasks [E5][E3][E6].
winnerExplanation: Google Gemini won because it had multiple meaningful model-specific mentions and most of them were concretely favorable—especially the strong enthusiasm for Gemma 4 running locally—while the other tracked providers were either mixed or discussed mainly through strategic, security, or product-UX complaints [E5][E3][E6].

openai.judgementSnippet: ChatGPT got a favorable one-shot math-paper mention, but a separate AI thread argued OpenAI’s lead is fragile and that it lacks durable distribution advantages [E1][E4].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Opus was singled out for initially failing a simple paper-following task, and Mythos was discussed mainly through the lens of cyber-risk briefings rather than useful product wins [E2][E8].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Gemma 4 drew strong praise as an unusually capable local coding model, while Gemini also got a straightforward success in a side-by-side math-paper test; the only softer note was that Gemini 2.5 Flash still was not seen as great at heavy tool use or large-context one-shots [E5][E3][E6].

microsoft_copilot.judgementSnippet: The Windows Copilot thread centered on cosmetic renaming, AI-first product priorities, and disappointment that M365 Copilot still cannot perform some mundane document-editing tasks users expected [E7].

evidence.summary: A top comment proposed the paper as an LLM test and said “ChatGPT(free) ... did it from the first try,” giving OpenAI a direct favorable comparison.
In the same comparison comment, “Opus(paid)” was said to treat the task as circular and only complete it after being told ChatGPT had already succeeded.
A benchmark-style top comment explicitly listed “Gemini - success” on the paper-following task.
In comments on Apple’s AI position, a user argued OpenAI’s initial lead had evaporated, called the economics a “death march,” and said OpenAI lacks the cash and distribution to keep up.
Comments on the Gemma 4 / Codex CLI story praised Gemma 4 26B as “an outlier in its weight class” and “the first time” a local coding setup felt reliably useful.
A comment compared Gemma 4 to Gemini 2.5 Flash, saying that level of model was good for code suggestions but still not great at broad tool use or large-context one-shot work.

Comments on the Windows 11 Copilot rename mocked the change as cosmetic, complained Microsoft puts AI into basic apps before fundamentals, and described M365 Copilot as unable to do obvious editing chores.
In the hacking-timeline thread, a top comment highlighted emergency briefings about cyber risks posed by Anthropic’s Mythos, framing the model as a security concern rather than a product win.
